mnemonics: Very Hungry Caterpillars Eat Apples (VHCEA) for the four group properties: V: Closure, H: Associativity, C: Identity, E: Inverses, A: Abelian (commutative) is a bonus property
keyPoints: A group is a set with an operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and inverses.
An Abelian group is a group where the operation is also commutative.
The identity element is unique for a given group, and each element has a unique inverse.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (Abelian); many important groups, like matrix multiplication, are not.
Forgetting to verify the identity element is in the set and actually functions as an identity for all elements.
Confusing the group operation with standard addition or multiplication, leading to incorrect application of properties.
